title: Pipeline FSM spec (descriptive registry)
description: Constrains spec/fsm.json — a DESCRIPTIVE mirror of the pipeline finite-state machine (states, the loop sub-machine, the T*/LT* transition table, guards and their fsm-state gate flags, and pointer-checked numeric constants). Diffed at dev time by scripts/spec_check.py (SC1–SC4) against references/state-machine.md §2/§3, references/self-learning-loops.md §1.3, the SKILL.md phase-gate prose, validate_run.py REQUIRED_GATES, and the *.schema.json constants it dereferences. This schema is NOT read by validate_run.py and adds NO constraint to the run-validation path (PRESERVES every guarantee). Sole authority over policy (e.g. LT7 is the unique back_edge) stays with spec_check; the schema only fixes the record SHAPE.

$comment.description: OPTIONAL. Free-form caveat that this file is descriptive/diffed, never enforced by validate_run.py.

note.description: OPTIONAL. Alias of $comment for the 'descriptive, not enforced' caveat.

states.description: The pipeline state ids (>=10). Plain string ids; the SC checker owns any membership/naming policy.

loop_states.description: The 6 correction-loop states (EXECUTE, VERIFY, ADJUDICATE, RETRY, ESCALATE, DONE). Enumerated because this set is fixed by the termination proof; spec_check still diffs it against self-learning-loops §1.3.

id.description: Transition id: T<n> (top-level pipeline edge) or LT<n> (loop edge).

guard_id.description: The id of a guard in guards[] that must hold, or null for an unguarded edge (e.g. the sole back_edge LT7 stays UNGUARDED — deadlock lesson 02/P1).

back_edge.description: true iff this edge is a back edge (loops toward an earlier state). Exactly one edge (LT7 RETRY->EXECUTE) is a back_edge; that count/identity policy is an SC concern, not a schema constraint.

$comment.description: OPTIONAL free-text annotation (not read by SC2's structural row-diff). Used e.g. on T10 to document the BGA amendment-fuel-exhaustion ESCALATE origin (WP6/B9).

transitions.description: The T*/LT* transition table, one closed object per edge.

gate_flag.description: The fsm-state gate flag this guard establishes (e.g. 'personas_selected'), or null if the guard sets no persisted flag.

required_from_phase.description: OPTIONAL. The state id from which this guard's gate flag is required (a member of states[]). Present when the guard establishes an fsm-state gate flag; SC3 diffs this against validate_run.py REQUIRED_GATES.

guards.description: The guard table (G-personas … G-signoff), each naming its gate flag.

pointer.description: A JSON Pointer into a schema, of the form '<schema-file>#/json/pointer' (e.g. 'fsm-state.schema.json#/properties/loop/properties/retries/maximum'). SC4 dereferences it and diffs the resolved value against expected_value. Only pointer-expressible constants live here — code-only invariants (I18 conservation, I4 ceiling, I16 panel logic) MUST NOT be recorded as a constant.

expected_value.description: The value expected at pointer (number/string/bool). SC4 asserts dereference(pointer) == expected_value.

constants.description: Pointer-checked constants: name + JSON Pointer into a schema + the expected value at that pointer.
